Patch Set 10: Code-Review-1

(6 comments)

Some significant amount of code simplification could be done in llog_cat_add_rec().

Should we be distinguishing the case of "filesystem out of space" (-ENOSPC) and "current llog is out of slots" (-ENOBUFS?)?  In the first case an error should be returned to the caller and the operation should (possibly) fail.  In the second case, the operation should retry and use the next llog file (unless the catalog is also full).

Patch Set 10:

Andreas, the problem with current approach is that we should use the objects we declared and nothing else - otherwise ZFS will panic in debug mode (which I think we'd like to support).

Patch Set 10:

Alex, sorry I don't understand what your comment implies.  Does that mean the current patch isn't correct, or that one of the changes that I've proposed will not work, or something else?

Patch Set 10:

I think that we can't retry with more than 1 plain llog: we have declared a write to the current llog and "next" one. IOW, we can't really loop over few llogs.
I'm not against a try to improve the code - do you want this to be done in the context on this specific bug or should we have another ticket?

Patch Set 10:

OK, I'm fine with not doing multiple retries, since the need for that is very unlikely.

Doing the "repeat once" loop in llog_cat_add_rec() would make sense as part of this patch, since this function is being changed anyway.

parents.subject: LU-7277 lod: keep trying to get remote update log

commit.subject: LU-6634 llog: destroy plain llog if init fails
commit.message:
LU-6634 llog: destroy plain llog if init fails

llog_cat_add_rec() should destroy the plain llog
in the same transaction if initialization of that
failed. also, llog_osd_write_rec() should check
the object still exists as it's possible that
another thread failed to initialize and destroyed
the llog.
